trade.summary: Germania exports machinery, vehicles, chemicals, and pharmaceuticals to the EAU. The EAU exports crude oil and re-exports goods. Post-2022 energy crisis, Germania accelerated LNG agreements with Gulf producers including the EAU, though volumes remain modest.

diplomatic.summary: Germania and the EAU maintain strong diplomatic relations supported by significant trade and investment ties. Germania has expressed concern over EAU human rights practices but maintains pragmatic engagement. The Gaza conflict (2023-24) strained some Arab relations with Western partners including Germania.

military.summary: Germania has sold defense equipment to the EAU including patrol vessels and communications systems, though German arms export policy sometimes restricts sales. Germania has no military bases in the EAU. Bundeswehr participated in Gulf security dialogues.

financial.summary: EAU sovereign wealth funds (ADIA, Mubadala) have significant German industrial and real estate investments. German companies (Siemens, BASF, Deutsche Bank) have major EAU operations. German-EAU investment corridor is well established.

energy.summary: Post-Russia energy shock, Germania signed LNG supply MoUs with ADNOC. RWE signed a gas agreement with the EAU in 2022. The EAU is becoming a more significant energy supplier to Germania as part of German energy diversification.

scientific.summary: German universities and research institutions collaborate with EAU counterparts in engineering, sustainability, and materials science. Fraunhofer and Max Planck institutes have EAU research partnerships.
